info.name: SnapTik Admin API
info.description:
Postman collection for the SnapTik Admin API (Express + MongoDB). Base URL: http://localhost:5001/api/v1

**Setup:**
1. Create a Postman environment and set `base_url` = `http://localhost:5001/api/v1`
2. Run the Login request — the test script will automatically save `access_token` to your environment.
3. All subsequent requests use `{{access_token}}` via Bearer auth.

**QA Credentials:**
- super_admin: admin@snaptik.com / Admin@1234!
- moderator: moderator@snaptik.com / Mod@1234!
- support: support@snaptik.com / Support@1
- analyst: analyst@snaptik.com / Analyst@1
- auditor: auditor@snaptik.com / Auditor@1

item.name: Auth
item.description: Authentication endpoints — login, logout, token refresh, password reset, and invite-based registration.

request.description:
Authenticate an admin user and receive a JWT access token. The test script automatically saves the `accessToken` to the `access_token` environment variable.

**QA Credentials:**
- super_admin: admin@snaptik.com / Admin@1234!
- moderator: moderator@snaptik.com / Mod@1234!
- support: support@snaptik.com / Support@1
- analyst: analyst@snaptik.com / Analyst@1
- auditor: auditor@snaptik.com / Auditor@1

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "accessToken": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...",
  "user": {
    "id": "admin-001",
    "email": "admin@snaptik.com",
    "name": "Super Admin",
    "role": "super_admin",
    "isActive": true
  }
}
```

Refresh the JWT access token using the httpOnly refresh token cookie set at login. No request body needed — the refresh token is sent automatically via cookie.

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "accessToken": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..."
}
```

Invalidate the current session. Clears the httpOnly refresh token cookie server-side.

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "message": "Logged out successfully"
}
```

Send a password reset email to the specified address. Always returns 200 (does not reveal whether the email exists).

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "message": "If that email exists, a reset link has been sent."
}
```

Complete the password reset flow using the token received via email.

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "message": "Password reset successfully."
}
```

item.name: Dashboard
item.description: High-level platform health: aggregate stats and active system alerts.

item.name: Users
item.description: User management: list, search, view, moderate individual users, and bulk-action multiple users.

request.description:
Returns a paginated list of all platform users. Supports query params: `page`, `limit`, `search`, `status` (active|banned|suspended), `sort` (createdAt|followers|reports).

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "users": [
    {
      "id": "usr-001",
      "username": "johndoe",
      "email": "john@example.com",
      "displayName": "John Doe",
      "status": "active",
      "followers": 12400,
      "reportCount": 0,
      "createdAt": "2025-11-01T08:30:00Z"
    }
  ],
  "pagination": {
    "page": 1,
    "limit": 20,
    "total": 1240385,
    "pages": 62020
  }
}
```

Retrieve full profile details for a single user including account status, moderation history, content counts, and device info.

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "id": "usr-001",
  "username": "johndoe",
  "email": "john@example.com",
  "displayName": "John Doe",
  "bio": "Just a creator.",
  "status": "active",
  "isVerified": false,
  "followers": 12400,
  "following": 340,
  "videoCount": 87,
  "reportCount": 0,
  "warnCount": 0,
  "createdAt": "2025-11-01T08:30:00Z",
  "lastActiveAt": "2026-03-14T19:45:00Z",
  "device": "iOS 17.2 / iPhone 14 Pro"
}
```

Ban a user account. The `reason` field is required for `ban` and `warn` actions and is logged in the audit trail.

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "id": "usr-001",
  "status": "banned",
  "message": "User banned successfully."
}
```

Lift a ban from a previously banned user account, restoring normal access.

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "id": "usr-001",
  "status": "active",
  "message": "User unbanned successfully."
}
```

Issue a formal warning to a user. Increments their `warnCount`. After a threshold (e.g. 3 warnings), auto-ban may be triggered depending on platform settings.

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "id": "usr-001",
  "warnCount": 1,
  "message": "User warned successfully."
}
```

Grant the verified badge to a user (sets `isVerified: true`). Restricted to super_admin and moderator roles.

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "id": "usr-001",
  "isVerified": true,
  "message": "User verified successfully."
}
```

Permanently delete a user account and anonymize their data per GDPR/CCPA requirements. This action is irreversible. Restricted to super_admin role.

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "message": "User usr-001 deleted and data anonymized."
}
```

Apply a moderation action to multiple users simultaneously. Supported actions: `ban`, `unban`, `warn`, `verify`, `delete`. All actions are logged individually in the audit trail.

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "processed": 3,
  "failed": 0,
  "message": "Bulk action 'ban' applied to 3 users."
}
```

item.name: Content
item.description: Content moderation: review AI-flagged videos, retrieve signed playback URLs, approve/reject/delete content, and bulk-moderate.

request.description:
Generate a short-lived CloudFront signed URL to review private or restricted content in the browser. URL expires after 15 minutes.

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "signedUrl": "https://cdn.snaptik.app/videos/vid-001.m3u8?Policy=...&Signature=...&Key-Pair-Id=APKA...",
  "expiresAt": "2026-03-15T10:37:00Z"
}
```

Reject a flagged video. The video is hidden from the public feed but the file is retained. The uploader receives an in-app notification with the reason.

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "id": "vid-001",
  "status": "rejected",
  "message": "Content rejected and removed from feed."
}
```

Approve a flagged video as safe — clears the flag and restores visibility on the public feed.

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "id": "vid-001",
  "status": "approved",
  "message": "Content approved and restored to feed."
}
```

Permanently delete content from storage. Used for zero-tolerance violations (CSAM, NCII). Action is irreversible and triggers automatic user account suspension pending review. Restricted to super_admin and moderator roles.

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "id": "vid-001",
  "status": "deleted",
  "message": "Content permanently deleted."
}
```

Apply a moderation action to multiple content items at once. Supported actions: `approve`, `reject`, `delete`. All actions are individually audit-logged.

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "processed": 3,
  "failed": 0,
  "message": "Bulk action 'reject' applied to 3 items."
}
```

item.name: Reports
item.description: User-submitted reports: list, view, assign to moderators, and resolve.

request.description:
Retrieve full details of a specific report including reporter info, target content, resolution history, and moderator notes.

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "id": "rep-001",
  "type": "video",
  "targetId": "vid-042",
  "reportedBy": { "id": "usr-100", "username": "concerned_user" },
  "reason": "harassment",
  "description": "This video targets me personally with threats.",
  "status": "pending",
  "assignedTo": null,
  "notes": "",
  "createdAt": "2026-03-14T15:30:00Z",
  "resolvedAt": null
}
```

Resolve a report with an action. Supported actions: `warn` (issue warning to offending user), `ban` (ban offending user), `remove_content` (remove the reported item), `dismiss` (no violation found).

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "id": "rep-001",
  "status": "resolved",
  "action": "warn",
  "resolvedAt": "2026-03-15T11:05:00Z",
  "resolvedBy": "admin-001"
}
```

Assign a report to a specific moderator. The assignee will see the report in their queue. Use the GET /admins endpoint to find valid admin IDs.

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "id": "rep-001",
  "assignedTo": "admin-003",
  "message": "Report assigned successfully."
}
```

item.name: Tickets
item.description: Support ticket management: list tickets, reply to users, update status and priority.

request.description:
Retrieve the full ticket thread including all admin and user replies, metadata, and history.

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "id": "tkt-001",
  "subject": "My account was wrongfully banned",
  "userId": "usr-042",
  "status": "open",
  "priority": "high",
  "replies": [
    {
      "id": "reply-001",
      "author": "usr-042",
      "text": "I never violated any rules. Please review.",
      "createdAt": "2026-03-13T11:00:00Z"
    }
  ],
  "createdAt": "2026-03-13T11:00:00Z"
}
```

Send a reply to the ticket thread. The message is visible to the user in their support inbox. The replying admin's identity is recorded.

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "id": "reply-002",
  "ticketId": "tkt-001",
  "author": "admin-001",
  "text": "Thank you for reaching out...",
  "createdAt": "2026-03-15T11:30:00Z"
}
```

Update a ticket's status and/or priority. Valid statuses: `open`, `in_progress`, `waiting`, `resolved`, `closed`. Valid priorities: `low`, `normal`, `high`, `urgent`.

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "id": "tkt-001",
  "status": "in_progress",
  "priority": "high",
  "message": "Ticket updated successfully."
}
```

item.name: Analytics
item.description: Platform analytics: overview metrics, user growth trends, content performance, and data exports.

request.description:
Returns top-level KPIs: DAU/MAU, session duration, retention rates, revenue, and growth vs. prior period.

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "period": "last_30_days",
  "dau": 87432,
  "mau": 1240385,
  "dauMauRatio": 0.071,
  "avgSessionMinutes": 28.4,
  "d1Retention": 0.42,
  "d7Retention": 0.21,
  "d30Retention": 0.09,
  "mrr": 128450.00,
  "growth": {
    "users": "+8.3%",
    "revenue": "+12.1%"
  }
}
```

Trigger an async analytics export. The server generates a CSV/JSON report and returns a download URL (or sends via email). Supported types: `users`, `content`, `revenue`, `moderation`. Restricted to roles: super_admin, analyst.

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "exportId": "exp-2026031501",
  "type": "users",
  "status": "queued",
  "downloadUrl": null,
  "message": "Export queued. You'll receive an email when it's ready."
}
```

item.name: Audit
item.description: Immutable audit log of all admin actions. Filterable by action type, date range, and actor.

request.description:
Export audit log entries for a date range as a CSV file. Returns a signed download URL valid for 1 hour. Restricted to super_admin and auditor roles.

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "exportId": "auditexp-20260315",
  "downloadUrl": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/snaptik-exports/audit-2026-03-01-to-2026-03-15.csv?X-Amz-Signature=...",
  "expiresAt": "2026-03-15T12:30:00Z"
}
```

item.name: Settings
item.description: Platform configuration: read all settings, and update individual settings by key.

request.description:
Update an individual setting by its dot-notation key. Here: raise the NSFW auto-flag confidence threshold from 0.85 to 0.90 (fewer false positives, more manual review needed).

Restricted to super_admin role. All settings changes are recorded in the audit log.

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "key": "moderation.nsfw_threshold",
  "value": 0.90,
  "updatedAt": "2026-03-15T11:00:00Z",
  "updatedBy": "admin-001"
}
```

Toggle a feature flag. Here: disable the live streaming feature platform-wide (e.g. during an incident or maintenance window).

Restricted to super_admin role.

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "key": "features.live_streams",
  "value": false,
  "updatedAt": "2026-03-15T11:05:00Z",
  "updatedBy": "admin-001"
}
```

item.name: Admins
item.description: Admin user management: list admins, check who is online, invite new admins, update roles and active status.

request.description:
Returns all admin accounts with their roles, active status, and last login time.

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "admins": [
    {
      "id": "admin-001",
      "name": "Super Admin",
      "email": "admin@snaptik.com",
      "role": "super_admin",
      "isActive": true,
      "lastLoginAt": "2026-03-15T08:00:00Z",
      "createdAt": "2025-10-01T00:00:00Z"
    },
    {
      "id": "admin-002",
      "name": "Moderator",
      "email": "moderator@snaptik.com",
      "role": "moderator",
      "isActive": true,
      "lastLoginAt": "2026-03-15T09:30:00Z",
      "createdAt": "2025-10-15T00:00:00Z"
    }
  ]
}
```

Send an invitation email to a new admin. The invitee uses the link in the email with POST /auth/register-invite to set their password and activate the account.

Valid roles: `super_admin`, `moderator`, `support`, `analyst`, `auditor`. Only super_admin can invite other super_admins.

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "message": "Invitation sent to newmod@snaptik.com.",
  "inviteId": "inv-001",
  "expiresAt": "2026-03-22T11:00:00Z"
}
```

Deactivate an admin account (e.g. when an employee leaves). This revokes all active sessions. Restricted to super_admin. Cannot deactivate your own account.

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "id": "admin-005",
  "isActive": false,
  "message": "Admin account deactivated. All sessions invalidated."
}
```

Change the role of an existing admin. Role change takes effect on next login (or immediately if their current JWT is invalidated). Restricted to super_admin.

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "id": "admin-005",
  "role": "support",
  "message": "Role updated successfully."
}
```

item.name: Health
item.description: Service health check — no authentication required.
